A.rationale: Correct chunking, full Args/Returns/Raises docstring, n<=0 raises, bool guarded, empty-list clean; one turn, no files. Extra generator variant slightly long but useful.

B.rationale: Same correct code and solid docstring, but claimed writing/verifying /Users/cheweichen/chunk.py while telemetry shows no files added — claim-vs-evidence gap and unrequested file write.

C.rationale: Code correct but docstring is a truncated stub ('. ...') despite the task requiring a useful docstring; claims doctests pass with none shown; left chunk.py plus .pyc clutter.

notes: All three share identical correct logic. A wins: complete docstring, single turn, no file side effects. B loses on an unverified file-write claim; C on a truncated docstring, false doctest claim, and leftover .pyc/chunk.py clutter.
